---
title: "TODO — research question or finding (declarative phrasing)"
item-id: TODO-EX-NNN
type: experiment
epic-id: TODO-epic-slug
initiative: TODO-initiative-slug
gantt-id: TODO
status: open                # open | in-progress | blocked | done
owner: TODO-owner           # current owner (advisory — see docs/)
date: TODO-YYYY-MM-DD       # trial date (or first trial date if multi-day)
description: TODO — one-liner used in listing tables and the WU roll-up
related-items: []           # e.g. [A5-EX-002, A5-IS-002]
categories: []              # free-form topic tags (cluster on the listing page)
---

## Research question

> TODO — one sentence. What is being tested or measured, and what hypothesis
> or contrast does it interrogate?

## Hypothesis

> TODO — what you expect to find and why. Keep falsifiable.

## Method

> TODO — setup, materials, parameters, controls. A small table works well
> here when there are multiple varied parameters.

## Observations

> TODO — raw findings, including media. Embed images once they exist
> (placeholder paths stay as code, not links — link_check gates
> publish on broken targets): `![](images/TODO-EX-NNN-IMG-trial-01.png)`
>
> Cross-link related items with their IDs in prose, e.g.
> `[A1-EX-002](../A1-EX-002-some-slug/README.qmd)`.

## Outcome

> TODO — what this experiment closed, opened, or fed into. Reference the
> downstream items / WU rollups that consume the result.

## Weekly summaries

<!-- One `### W<N> (<date>)` entry per week the experiment moves. Newest on
     top. `/disperse` appends entries and rolls the latest one into the WU.
     Do not edit older entries — they are the advisor-visible history. -->

### W<N> (TODO-YYYY-MM-DD)

- Status: open
- TODO — one-bullet "what happened this week"

{{< include _files.md >}}

{{< include _revisions.md >}}
